name: Security Foundations

architecture_decisions.decision: Use role and scope based authorization across all private resources.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Trip membership, organizer control, private event participant access, public supporter access, and admin access are distinct.
Financial references, ledgers, receipt corrections, membership, visibility, and moderation require traceability.

security_foundations.authentication: Authenticated accounts for organizers, members, and admins; public supporters can view public trip pages and leave the app for external-provider checkout.
scope_rules: Trip private data requires approved membership.
Private event data requires event participation or admin access.
Public content requires explicit organizer publication.
External contribution provider references are organizer/admin visible unless intentionally summarized publicly.
Receipt and media access follows trip or event scope.
controls: rate limits for uploads, invites, reports, and provider sync

risks_and_constraints.risk: Visibility bugs could leak private trip, event, receipt, or media data.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Centralize visibility policy and test all public/private/event participant transitions.

risks_and_constraints.risk: External contribution references may contain provider metadata that should not be public.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Store provider metadata separately from public contribution summaries.

stage_4_handoff_notes: Design must show clear access states and avoid exposing private context on public pages.
stage_5_handoff_notes: Add authorization and audit-log tests to every sensitive workflow ticket.
